title: Case 3 — the tool says 'nothing here'
demonstrates: The case that earns trust. Adverse risk, no targeted therapy, no valid MRD marker. It also exercises both fall-through paths: SETBP1 should miss the corpus and be picked up live by CIViC, and SMC3 should miss both — and must appear under 'Not assessed' rather than quietly vanishing.
clinical_problem: Pancytopenia found on routine bloods. Breast cancer six years ago, treated with anthracycline-based chemotherapy and adjuvant radiotherapy; in remission. Three months of increasing lethargy.
film: Pancytopenic. 8% circulating blasts. Marked dysplasia, dimorphic red cells, hypogranular neutrophils.
marrow_diagnosis: Acute myeloid leukaemia, therapy-related (prior cytotoxic therapy). Blasts 34%. Karyotype: complex, 5 abnormalities including del(5q), -7, del(17p).

ngs.note: VAF 68% with del(17p) — consistent with biallelic loss
